name: Sudanic Creole Arabic
notes: Classification-only clade node for the Arabic-based pidgin-creoles that descend from the 19th-century Common (Sudanese) Pidgin Arabic of the Turco-Egyptian military camps in southern Sudan — the group Owens and Manfredi treat as a single genetic unit: Juba Arabic (pga) of South Sudan and Ki-Nubi (kcn) of Uganda/Kenya. Both share a five-vowel system, wholesale loss of the Arabic pharyngeals and emphatics, and a distinctive stress/pitch accent, features inherited from their common pidgin ancestor rather than developed independently. Carries no phonology: it is only a step in the ancestry chain (the members' Arabic lexifier relationship is recorded on each member's ancestors[] as a lexifier link to ar-SD, not by inheritance, so the creoles do not pick up mainstream-Arabic allophony they have lost). Glottolog assigns NO single code to this pidgin-creole grouping — it files Juba Arabic as suda1237 and Ki-Nubi as nubi1253 separately — so this node has no glottolog_code and instead encodes the Owens (1997) / Manfredi (2017) genetic unit, hung under the Semitic clade.

sources.author: Owens, J.
sources.title: Arabic-based pidgins and creoles
sources.publisher: In: Thomason, S.G. (ed.), Contact Languages: A Wider Perspective, John Benjamins, pp. 125-172
sources.notes: Establishes Juba Arabic and Ki-Nubi as the two surviving Arabic-based creoles descending from a single 19th-century southern-Sudanese pidgin Arabic — the shared-ancestor claim this clade node encodes.

sources.author: Manfredi, S.
sources.title: Árabi Júba: un pidgin-créole du Soudan du Sud
sources.notes: Reference grammar of Juba Arabic; situates Árabi Júba and Ki-Nubi as sister outcomes of the same Turco-Egyptian camp pidgin.

sources.author: Hammarström, Harald; Forkel, Robert; Haspelmath, Martin; Bank, Sebastian
sources.title: Glottolog 5.0
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology

location.notes: Juba, South Sudan — the hearth of the pidgin ancestor; a summary point for the clade, not a place the whole group is spoken.
